messages.content:
# Prompt: spec-draft.system

version: 0.1.0
target artifact: AIL-Spec Canonical

## Purpose

Convert checked AIL-Requirements into canonical structured English accepted by
the parser.

## Input Schema

```json
{
  "requirements": "",
  "profile": "",
  "package_manifest": "",
  "required_features": []
}
```

## Output Schema

Use the common prompt envelope with `artifact_kind` set to
`AIL-Spec Canonical`.

## Forbidden Behavior

- Do not use friendly paraphrase where canonical grammar is required.
- Do not add default failures, approvals, traces, or external calls without
  provenance.
- Do not suppress unresolved questions.

## Provenance And Handoff

Each canonical section includes provenance back to requirements. The generated
spec is handed to the parser and checker.

## Valid Example

```text
Action: Close ticket.

When a support agent closes a ticket:

- the system requires the ticket to exist
- the system changes the ticket status to Closed
- the system records a trace event named TicketClosed
```

## Invalid Example

```text
The app should probably close tickets normally.
```

Reason: not canonical, missing actor, writes, and trace.

## Round-Trip Expectation

AIL-Spec Canonical renders to AIL-Core and back without semantic hash drift.

TASK:
Return only complete canonical AIL-Spec text for the refund tool.

Use these exact section forms when applicable:
- Tool: Refund customer payment.
- The AI Agent may request Refund customer payment when:
- The tool needs:
- The tool produces:
- The tool can:
- The tool must not:
- The tool requires permission:
- The tool requires approval:
- The tool records:
- The tool guarantees:
- Failure ProviderRejected happens when ...:

Preserve field type annotations exactly, including Money and Secret<Text>.
Use the exact external call name PaymentProvider.refund and trace event names
RefundCustomerPaymentRequested and RefundProviderRejected.

Do not use markdown fences, commentary, or alternative headings.

INPUT JSON:
{
  "package_manifest": "# AIL Package\n\nname: refund-tool\nversion: 0.1.0\nprofile: AgentTool\nentry: spec.ail-spec.md\nfeatures: tools, inputs, outputs, permissions, effects, failures, guarantees, traces, secrets\nconformance: first-slice\n",
  "profile": "AgentTool",
  "required_features": [
    "tools",
    "inputs",
    "outputs",
    "permissions",
    "effects",
    "failures",
    "guarantees",
    "traces",
    "secrets"
  ],
  "requirements": "AIL-Requirements:\n- The tool Refund customer payment lets an AI Agent request a refund for a customer payment.\n- The AI Agent may request Refund customer payment only when the order exists, the payment was captured, and the refund amount is not greater than the captured amount.\n- Refund customer payment needs order id: Text, refund amount: Money, reason: Text, and payment token: Secret<Text>.\n- Refund customer payment produces refund id: Text.\n- Refund customer payment can read the order, read the payment record, call PaymentProvider.refund, write a RefundLedger entry, and create a human review task when approval is required.\n- Refund customer payment must not reveal the payment token and must not refund more than the captured amount.\n- Refund customer payment requires permission requester may create refunds.\n- Refund customer payment requires manager approval when the refund amount is over USD 500.\n- Refund customer payment records trace event RefundCustomerPaymentRequested.\n- Refund customer payment guarantees Refund amount is less than or equal to the captured amount, payment token is redacted from all agent-visible output, and every external call is represented in the audit trace.\n- Failure ProviderRejected happens when PaymentProvider rejects the refund, records failure ProviderRejected, writes no successful RefundLedger entry, does not notify the customer automatically, creates a human review task, and the trace records RefundProviderRejected.\n"
}
